question.text: Deepak, aged 82, is a resident individual who opts for the old tax regime for FY 2025-26. What is his basic exemption limit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the old tax regime, basic exemption limits vary by age: (1) Individuals below 60 years: Rs. 2,50,000; (2) Senior citizens aged 60-79: Rs. 3,00,000; (3) Super senior citizens aged 80 and above: Rs. 5,00,000. Since Deepak is 82, he qualifies as a super senior citizen with an exemption limit of Rs. 5,00,000. **The Trap:** Rs. 2,50,000 is for individuals below 60. Rs. 3,00,000 is for senior citizens (60-79) — candidates may confuse the age cutoffs. Rs. 4,00,000 is the nil-tax slab under the new regime (FY 2025-26), not the old regime exemption — a common mix-up between regimes. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A tests age-based exemption limits when recommending tax-efficient investments for senior citizens. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify the client's age as of March 31 of the relevant PY — the classification as senior or super senior citizen is determined on the last day of the financial year, not on the date of investment.
